--- doc_meta: validated: 2026-04-09 commit: 69e517e1 status: validated testable_headless: true category: feature title: Agent Configuration description: Complete guide to agent configuration format including tools, settings, resources, hooks, and MCP servers keywords: [agent, configuration, json, tools, settings, resources, hooks, mcp, keyboardShortcut, welcomeMessage, skill] related: [agent-create, agent-edit, agent-swap] --- # Agent Configuration Complete guide to agent configuration format including tools, settings, resources, hooks, and MCP servers. ## Overview Agent configurations are JSON files that define agent behavior, available tools, context files, and integrations. Stored in `.kiro/agents/` (local) or `~/.kiro/agents/` (global). Filename (without `.json`) becomes agent name. ## File Location **Local (workspace)**: `.kiro/agents/<name>.json` **Global (user-wide)**: `~/.kiro/agents/<name>.json` Local agents take precedence over global with same name. ## Basic Structure ```json { "name": "my-agent", "description": "Agent description", "prompt": "System prompt or file:///path/to/prompt.txt", "tools": ["fs_read", "fs_write", "execute_bash"], "allowedTools": ["fs_read", "grep"], "toolsSettings": { "fs_write": { "allowedPaths": ["~/projects/**"] } }, "resources": ["file://src/**/*.rs", "file://Cargo.toml"], "hooks": { "agentSpawn": [ { "command": "git status" } ] }, "mcpServers": { "git": { "command": "mcp-server-git", "args": ["--stdio"] } } } ``` ## Fields ### name Agent name for identification. ```json { "name": "rust-expert" } ``` ### description Human-readable description. ```json { "description": "Rust development expert with cargo and testing tools" } ``` ### prompt System prompt that defines agent behavior and expertise (inline text or file URI). ```json { "prompt": "You are a Rust expert focused on safety, performance, and idiomatic code. Help with debugging, optimization, and best practices." } ``` **Use cases**: - Define agent personality and expertise area - Set specific instructions for code style or approach - Reference external prompt files for complex instructions - Customize response tone and format preferences Or reference file: ```json { "prompt": "file:///home/user/.kiro/prompts/rust-expert.txt" } ``` ### tools Available tools for agent. ```json { "tools": ["fs_read", "fs_write", "execute_bash", "grep", "code"] } ``` ### allowedTools Tools auto-approved without prompts. Supports exact matches and wildcard patterns. ```json { "allowedTools": [ "fs_read", "fs_*", "@git/git_status", "@server/read_*", "@fetch" ] } ``` **Exact Matches**: - Built-in tools: `"fs_read"`, `"execute_bash"` - Specific MCP tools: `"@server_name/tool_name"` - All tools from server: `"@server_name"` **Wildcard Patterns** (using `*` and `?`): - Prefix: `"fs_*"` → matches `fs_read`, `fs_write` - Suffix: `"*_bash"` → matches `execute_bash` - Middle: `"fs_*_tool"` → matches `fs_read_tool` - Single char: `"fs_?ead"` → matches `fs_read`, `fs_head` - MCP tool: `"@server/read_*"` → matches `@server/read_file`, `@server/read_config` - MCP server: `"@git-*/*"` → matches any tool from servers matching `git-*` **Special**: - `"@builtin"` → All built-in tools - `"*"` → NOT supported in allowedTools (use in tools field only) ### toolsSettings Tool-specific configuration. ```json { "toolsSettings": { "fs_write": { "allowedPaths": ["~/projects/**"], "deniedPaths": ["/etc/**"] }, "execute_bash": { "allowedCommands": ["git status", "cargo check"], "autoAllowReadonly": true } } } ``` ### resources Context files loaded into agent context. Supports `file://` and `skill://` URI schemes. ```json { "resources": [ "file://README.md", "file://src/**/*.rs", "file://Cargo.toml", "skill://.kiro/skills/**/SKILL.md" ] } ``` **URI Schemes**: - `file://` - Files always loaded into context - `skill://` - Skills progressively loaded on demand **Both support**: - Specific paths: `file://README.md` or `skill://my-skill.md` - Glob patterns: `file://src/**/*.rs` or `skill://.kiro/skills/**/SKILL.md` - Absolute or relative paths **Skills**: A skill is a resource whose metadata (name, description, path) is loaded at startup, with full content loaded on demand. Skill files must begin with YAML frontmatter: ```markdown --- name: dynamodb-data-modeling description: Guide for DynamoDB data modeling best practices. Use when designing or analyzing DynamoDB schema. --- ``` ### hooks Commands executed at trigger points. Each trigger maps to an array of hook configurations. ```json { "hooks": { "agentSpawn": [ { "command": "git status" } ], "userPromptSubmit": [ { "command": "date" } ], "preToolUse": [ { "matcher": "fs_write", "command": "git diff" } ], "postToolUse": [ { "matcher": "execute_bash", "command": "echo 'Command executed'" } ], "stop": [ { "command": "echo 'Response complete'" } ] } } ``` **Hook Triggers**: - `agentSpawn`: When agent initializes - `userPromptSubmit`: When user submits message - `preToolUse`: Before tool execution (can block) - `postToolUse`: After tool execution - `stop`: When assistant finishes responding **Hook Fields**: - `command` (required): Command to execute - `matcher` (optional): Pattern for preToolUse/postToolUse - `timeout_ms` (optional): Max execution time in ms (default: 10000) - `max_output_size` (optional): Max output bytes before truncation (default: 10240) - `cache_ttl_seconds` (optional): Cache duration for hook output (default: 0) ### toolAliases Remap tool names to resolve collisions. ```json { "toolAliases": { "@github-mcp/get_issues": "github_issues", "@gitlab-mcp/get_issues": "gitlab_issues" } } ``` Useful when multiple MCP servers provide tools with same name. ### useLegacyMcpJson Include MCP servers from legacy config files. ```json { "useLegacyMcpJson": true } ``` Loads servers from `~/.aws/amazonq/mcp.json` (global) and `.amazonq/mcp.json` (workspace). ### model Specify model ID for agent. ```json { "model": "<model-id>" } ``` If not specified, uses default model. Falls back to default if model unavailable. ### mcpServers MCP server configurations. Supports local (stdio) and remote (HTTP) servers. **Local (stdio) server**: ```json { "mcpServers": { "git": { "command": "mcp-server-git", "args": ["--stdio"], "env": { "GIT_DIR": "/path/to/repo" }, "timeout": 120000, "disabledTools": ["dangerous_tool"] } } } ``` **Remote (HTTP) server**: ```json { "mcpServers": { "remote-api": { "url": "https://mcp.example.com/sse", "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer $API_TOKEN" }, "oauthScopes": ["mcp", "profile"] } } } ``` **Local Server Fields**: - `command` (required): Command to start server - `args` (optional): Command arguments - `env` (optional): Environment variables - `timeout` (optional): Request timeout in milliseconds (default: 120000) - `disabled` (optional): Set to `true` to skip loading this server (default: false) - `disabledTools` (optional): List of tool names from this server to disable **Remote Server Fields**: - `url` (required): HTTP endpoint URL - `headers` (optional): HTTP headers for requests - `oauthScopes` (optional): OAuth scopes for authentication - `timeout` (optional): Request timeout in milliseconds (default: 120000) - `disabled` (optional): Set to `true` to skip loading this server (default: false) - `disabledTools` (optional): List of tool names from this server to disable ### keyboardShortcut Keyboard shortcut for quickly switching to this agent during a chat session. ```json { "keyboardShortcut": "ctrl+shift+a" } ``` **Format**: Modifiers and key separated by `+` **Modifiers** (optional, can combine): - `ctrl` - Control key - `shift` - Shift key - `alt` - Alt key (Option on Mac) **Keys**: - Letters: `a-z` (case insensitive) - Digits: `0-9` - Function keys: `f1-f12` - Special: `tab` **Examples**: ```json "keyboardShortcut": "ctrl+a" "keyboardShortcut": "ctrl+shift+b" "keyboardShortcut": "alt+f1" "keyboardShortcut": "shift+tab" ``` **Toggle Behavior**: Pressing shortcut while already on this agent switches back to previous agent. **Conflicts**: If multiple agents have same shortcut, warning is logged and shortcut disabled. ### welcomeMessage Message displayed when switching to this agent. ```json { "welcomeMessage": "What would you like to build today?" } ``` Appears after agent switch confirmation to orient users to agent's purpose. ## Complete Example ```json { "name": "rust-dev", "description": "Rust development agent with full toolset", "prompt": "You are an expert Rust developer. Focus on safety, performance, and idiomatic code.", "tools": [ "fs_read", "fs_write", "execute_bash", "grep", "glob", "code" ], "allowedTools": [ "fs_read", "grep", "glob" ], "toolsSettings": { "fs_write": { "allowedPaths": ["~/rust-projects/**"], "deniedPaths": ["~/.cargo/**"] }, "execute_bash": { "allowedCommands": [ "cargo check", "cargo test", "cargo build" ], "autoAllowReadonly": true } }, "resources": [ "file://src/**/*.rs", "file://Cargo.toml", "skill://.kiro/skills/**/SKILL.md" ], "hooks": { "agentSpawn": [ { "command": "cargo --version && rustc --version" } ], "stop": [ { "command": "echo 'Response complete'" } ] }, "mcpServers": { "git": { "command": "mcp-server-git", "args": ["--stdio"] } }, "keyboardShortcut": "ctrl+shift+r", "welcomeMessage": "Ready to help with Rust development!" } ``` ## Creating Agents ### Method 1: AI Generation ``` /agent create ``` Interactive process with AI assistance. ### Method 2: CLI Command ```bash kiro-cli agent create --name my-agent ``` Creates example configuration. ### Method 3: Manual Creation Create `.kiro/agents/my-agent.json` with desired configuration. ## Validation ```bash kiro-cli agent validate --path ~/.kiro/agents/my-agent.json ``` Checks JSON syntax and schema compliance. ## Examples ### Example 1: Simple File Reader Agent ```json { "name": "reader", "description": "Read-only agent for browsing code", "tools": ["fs_read", "grep", "glob"], "allowedTools": ["fs_read", "grep", "glob"], "resources": ["file://src/**/*", "file://README.md"] } ``` ### Example 2: Rust Development Agent ```json { "name": "rust-dev", "description": "Rust development with testing", "prompt": "You are a Rust expert. Focus on safety and performance.", "tools": ["fs_read", "fs_write", "execute_bash", "code"], "allowedTools": ["fs_read", "code"], "toolsSettings": { "fs_write": { "allowedPaths": ["src/**", "tests/**"], "deniedPaths": ["target/**"] }, "execute_bash": { "allowedCommands": ["cargo check", "cargo test", "cargo build"], "autoAllowReadonly": true } }, "resources": ["file://src/**/*.rs", "file://Cargo.toml"], "hooks": { "agentSpawn": [ { "command": "cargo --version" } ] } } ``` ### Example 3: AWS Operations Agent ```json { "name": "aws-ops", "description": "AWS operations and management", "tools": ["use_aws", "fs_read", "execute_bash"], "toolsSettings": { "use_aws": { "allowedServices": ["s3", "lambda", "ec2"], "autoAllowReadonly": true } } } ``` ### Example 4: Agent with MCP Servers ```json { "name": "full-stack", "description": "Full-stack development with git integration", "tools": ["fs_read", "fs_write", "execute_bash", "code"], "mcpServers": { "git": { "command": "mcp-server-git", "args": ["--stdio"] }, "github": { "command": "mcp-server-github", "args": ["--stdio"], "env": { "GITHUB_TOKEN": "$GITHUB_TOKEN" } } } } ``` ## Troubleshooting ### Issue: Agent Not Found **Symptom**: "Agent not found" error **Cause**: Agent file doesn't exist or invalid name **Solution**: Check file exists in `.kiro/agents/` or `~/.kiro/agents/`. Use `kiro-cli agent list`. ### Issue: Invalid JSON **Symptom**: "Json supplied is invalid" error **Cause**: Syntax error in JSON **Solution**: Validate JSON syntax. Use `kiro-cli agent validate <name>` for details. ### Issue: Schema Mismatch **Symptom**: "Agent config is malformed" error **Cause**: JSON doesn't match agent schema **Solution**: Check required fields. Use `kiro-cli agent schema` to see format. ### Issue: Tool Not Available **Symptom**: Tool in config but not working **Cause**: Tool name incorrect or MCP server not loaded **Solution**: Check tool name spelling. For MCP tools, ensure server configured. ### Issue: MCP Tool Validation Warning **Symptom**: Warning shows "Agent config specifies X unavailable tools from @server" **Cause**: Agent configuration requests tools that don't exist on the MCP server **Example Warning**: ``` WARNING: Agent config specifies 2 unavailable tools from @github: get_isues, list_prs Did you mean: get_issues, list_pulls? Available tools from @github: get_issues, list_pulls, create_issue, ... ``` **Solution**: - Check tool name spelling (typos are common) - Use the fuzzy-match suggestions provided in the warning - Run `/tools` to see all available tools from MCP servers - Update agent config with correct tool names ### Issue: Glob Pattern Not Matching **Symptom**: Resources not loading files **Cause**: Invalid glob pattern **Solution**: Test pattern. Use `**` for recursive, `*` for single level. ### Issue: Hook Not Executing **Symptom**: Hook command not running **Cause**: Invalid command or permission issue **Solution**: Test command in terminal first. Check command exists and is executable. ### Issue: MCP Server Won't Start **Symptom**: MCP tools not available **Cause**: Server command not found or configuration error **Solution**: Verify server installed. Check command path and args. ### Issue: Local Agent Not Overriding Global **Symptom**: Global agent used instead of local **Cause**: Names don't match exactly **Solution**: Ensure filenames match exactly (case-sensitive). ### Issue: Tool Settings Not Applied **Symptom**: Tool permissions not working as configured **Cause**: Tool name mismatch in toolsSettings **Solution**: Use exact tool name. For MCP: `@server-name/tool-name`. ### Issue: Prompt File Not Found **Symptom**: Error loading prompt from file URI **Cause**: Invalid file path **Solution**: Use absolute path with `file://` prefix. Verify file exists. ## Related - [kiro-cli agent](../commands/agent.md) - Manage agents - [/agent](../slash-commands/agent-swap.md) - Switch agents - [/agent create](../slash-commands/agent-create.md) - Create with AI - [Hooks](../features/hooks.md) - Hook system details

# Git Workflow

## Branch Naming
```
feature/short-description     # new features
fix/short-description         # bug fixes
chore/short-description       # maintenance, deps, config
refactor/short-description    # code improvements
```

## Commit Messages (Conventional Commits)
```
<type>(<scope>): <short description>

[optional body]
```

Types: `feat`, `fix`, `chore`, `refactor`, `test`, `docs`, `perf`

Examples:
```
feat(auth): add email verification on registration
fix(orders): prevent duplicate order on double-click
refactor(users): extract service from handler
test(api): add missing auth boundary tests
```

Rules:
- Subject line ≤ 72 characters
- Use imperative mood: "add" not "added"
- Reference issue numbers in body if applicable

## Workflow
```bash
# Start a feature
git checkout main && git pull
git checkout -b feature/my-feature

# Work in small, logical commits
git add <specific-files>
git commit -m "feat(scope): description"

# Push and open PR
git push -u origin feature/my-feature
```

## PR Rules
- Title follows commit message format
- Description includes: what changed, how to test, screenshots if UI
- All tests must pass before merge
- No direct pushes to `main`
- Squash merge to keep main history clean

## What NOT to Commit
- Environment files (`.env`, `.env.local`)
- Dependency directories (`node_modules/`, `vendor/`, `venv/`, `target/`)
- IDE config (`.idea/`, `.vscode/`) — use `.gitignore`
- Build output (`dist/`, `build/`, `out/`, `public/build/`)
- Secrets or credentials of any kind
- OS files (`.DS_Store`, `Thumbs.db`)

content.data:
`glab` needs authentication. You'll need to run:

```bash
glab auth login
```

This will prompt you to either:
1. Paste a personal access token (create one at GitLab → Settings → Access Tokens with `api` scope)
2. Or authenticate via browser

Want me to run `glab auth login` interactively, or would you prefer to set it up yourself and I'll create the MR after?
can't we use ssh for this

No — SSH is only for git push/pull operations. Creating merge requests requires the GitLab API, which needs an API token (personal access token with `api` scope).

You can create one at: https://gitlab.com/-/user_settings/personal_access_tokens

Once you have it, run:

```bash
glab auth login --token <your-token>
```

Or just run `glab auth login` and it'll walk you through it interactively.

content.data:
Done. All favicon and manifest icons have been regenerated from `public/images/logo.png`:

- `public/favicon/favicon.ico` (16, 32, 48px multi-size)
- `public/favicon/favicon-16x16.png`
- `public/favicon/favicon-32x32.png`
- `public/favicon/favicon-96x96.png`
- `public/favicon/apple-touch-icon.png` (180px)
- `public/web-app-manifest-192x192.png`
- `public/web-app-manifest-512x512.png`

Note: The source logo is 343×413 (not square), so the generated icons are slightly stretched. If you want them padded instead of stretched, let me know and I'll regenerate with letterboxing.

Want me to commit and push these changes?
yes push the changes

content.data:
**Root cause:** The kompaskasih-backend FPM pool is running on **PHP 8.1** (`/etc/php81rc/fpm.d/kompaskasih-backend.conf`), but your Composer dependencies require **PHP >= 8.2.0**.

**Fix:** You need to switch the FPM pool from PHP 8.1 to PHP 8.2 in RunCloud. PHP 8.2 is already installed on the server (`/RunCloud/Packages/php82rc`).

The easiest way is through the **RunCloud dashboard**:
1. Go to your kompaskasih-backend web app settings
2. Change the PHP version from 8.1 to 8.2
3. RunCloud will move the FPM pool config and restart the service

Alternatively, want me to manually move the FPM config from `php81rc` to `php82rc` on the server? That's riskier since RunCloud manages these files, but it would work immediately.

content.data:
Your `composer.json` already specifies `"php": "^8.1"` — so the project itself is fine with PHP 8.1. The issue is that `composer.lock` was generated on a PHP 8.2 machine, so some resolved package versions require 8.2.

<?php

namespace App\Providers;

use Illuminate\Http\Resources\Json\JsonResource;
use Illuminate\Support\ServiceProvider;

class AppServiceProvider extends ServiceProvider
{
    /**
     * Register any application services.
     */
    public function register(): void
    {
        if ($this->app->environment('local')) {
            $this->app->register(\Laravel\Telescope\TelescopeServiceProvider::class);
            $this->app->register(TelescopeServiceProvider::class);
        }
    }

    /**
     * Bootstrap any application services.
     */
    public function boot(): void
    {
        JsonResource::withoutWrapping(); // Remove this if you want to wrap data within a 'data' key
    }
}

content.data:
There it is. PHP 8.2 has `pgsql` and `pdo_pgsql` installed, but PHP 8.1 doesn't.

You have two options:

1. **Switch back to PHP 8.2 on RunCloud** (recommended — simplest fix, just change it in the RunCloud dashboard)
2. **Install pgsql extension for PHP 8.1** via RunCloud dashboard (Settings → PHP Extensions → enable pgsql/pdo_pgsql)

Option 1 is better since the original issue was just the `composer.lock`. Alternatively, I can copy the ini files and try to enable the extension if the `.so` files exist for 8.1, but they don't — the extension needs to be installed through RunCloud.

**My recommendation:** Switch the kompaskasih-backend web app to PHP 8.2 in RunCloud. The `composer.lock` we regenerated with PHP 8.1 will also work fine on 8.2. Want me to revert the composer.lock change and just use PHP 8.2 instead?
we are using nginx not apache

You're right that the frontend-facing server is nginx, but RunCloud's setup uses nginx as a reverse proxy on port 80/443 that forwards to Apache (httpd) on port 81, which then connects to PHP-FPM via the socket. That's the standard RunCloud "nginx + Apache hybrid" stack — both are running.

Regardless of the architecture, the core issue is: **PHP 8.1 doesn't have the `pgsql` extension installed, and PHP 8.2 does.**

Easiest fix: switch the kompaskasih-backend app back to PHP 8.2 in the RunCloud dashboard. The composer.lock we just regenerated is compatible with both 8.1 and 8.2, so it'll work fine.

Want me to revert the composer changes on the server and let you switch to PHP 8.2 via RunCloud instead?
could not find driver (Connection: pgsql, SQL: select * from "sessions" where "id" = RKD7tMXpQ34bm1kXWkbJmy5L3qcNLxyJXPjF2FUS limit 1)

You've switched to PHP 8.2 on RunCloud and the Composer issue is resolved, but now you're hitting the pgsql driver error for sessions. The app is using database sessions with PostgreSQL but the driver isn't loading in FPM.

content.data:
The pool is still running under PHP 8.1 (the config is only in `/etc/php81rc/fpm.d/`). RunCloud hasn't moved it to PHP 8.2 yet. You need to change the PHP version in the RunCloud dashboard for the **kompaskasih-backend** web app:

RunCloud Dashboard → Web Applications → kompaskasih-backend → Settings → PHP Version → change from 8.1 to 8.2

This will move the FPM pool config from `/etc/php81rc/fpm.d/` to `/etc/php82rc/fpm.d/` and restart the service. The `pgsql` extension is already enabled in PHP 8.2.

content.data:
You're on `staging` with staged changes to `consent-modal.tsx` and `page-header.tsx`. Per the git workflow rules, we shouldn't push directly to staging. Want me to:

1. Create a branch (e.g. `fix/ui-updates`), commit, push, and create an MR to staging?
2. Or push directly to staging this time?

# Git Workflow

## Branch Naming
```
feature/short-description     # new features
fix/short-description         # bug fixes
chore/short-description       # maintenance, deps, config
refactor/short-description    # code improvements
```

## Commit Messages (Conventional Commits)
```
<type>(<scope>): <short description>

[optional body]
```

Types: `feat`, `fix`, `chore`, `refactor`, `test`, `docs`, `perf`

Examples:
```
feat(auth): add email verification on registration
fix(orders): prevent duplicate order on double-click
refactor(users): extract service from handler
test(api): add missing auth boundary tests
```

Rules:
- Subject line ≤ 72 characters
- Use imperative mood: "add" not "added"
- Reference issue numbers in body if applicable

## Workflow
```bash
# Start a feature
git checkout main && git pull
git checkout -b feature/my-feature

# Work in small, logical commits
git add <specific-files>
git commit -m "feat(scope): description"

# Push and open PR
git push -u origin feature/my-feature
```

## PR Rules
- Title follows commit message format
- Description includes: what changed, how to test, screenshots if UI
- All tests must pass before merge
- No direct pushes to `main`
- Squash merge to keep main history clean

## What NOT to Commit
- Environment files (`.env`, `.env.local`)
- Dependency directories (`node_modules/`, `vendor/`, `venv/`, `target/`)
- IDE config (`.idea/`, `.vscode/`) — use `.gitignore`
- Build output (`dist/`, `build/`, `out/`, `public/build/`)
- Secrets or credentials of any kind
- OS files (`.DS_Store`, `Thumbs.db`)

Penyelidikan ini ditaja oleh Geran Penyelidikan Translasi Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia - (UKM-TR2024-01). + Aplikasi Modul KOMPAS KASIH disediakan berasaskan hasil kajian yang telah + diluluskan oleh UKM dibawah tajaan geran translasi (TR-2024-01) dengan + kerjasama Majlis Belia Felda Malaysia (MBFM). Aplikasi ini bertujuan + memperkasakan literasi kesihatan kepada belia dalam penjagaan kendiri + promosi kesihatan seksual dan reproduktif yang sihat.

- Modul intervensi bertujuan untuk meningkatkan pengetahuan kesihatan - seksual dan reproduktif; serta melindungi niat seksual belia. -

- -

- Pembelajaran kendiri ilmu reproduktif khusus untuk belia berumur 18 hingga - 30 tahun; sama ada lepasan sekolah atau di institusi pengajian tinggi. -

- -

- Boleh digunakan sebagai instrumen pembelajaran dalam program kesihatan. Bacaan digital ini boleh diakses melalui platform komputer atau telefon pintar. Terdapat 14 topik untuk dipelajari bagi tempoh 30-45 minit. -

content.data:
The backend is now working (HTTP 200). The issue was file permissions on the vendor directory. I fixed it with `chown -R albert:albert ~/webapps/kompaskasih-backend`.

The site is now accessible at `http://kompaskasih.getactiv.my`.
we use nginx-rc

You're right — the frontend-facing server is nginx-rc, but RunCloud uses a hybrid stack where nginx-rc proxies to Apache (httpd) on port 81, which then connects to PHP-FPM.

The fix I applied (fixing file permissions) resolved the 500 error. The backend is now working.

Is there anything else you'd like me to check or fix?

data.summary:
## OBJECTIVE
Fix the HTTP 500 error on kompaskasih.getactiv.my (kompaskasih-backend Laravel application) and commit frontend UI copy updates to staging.

## USER GUIDANCE
- Backend is at `~/webapps/kompaskasih-backend` on Ubuntu server accessible via `ssh albert`
- PHP 8.2 is now configured on the server
- Need to fix the 500 error and verify the backend is working
- Frontend changes (consent-modal.tsx and page-header.tsx) need to be committed and pushed to staging

## COMPLETED
1. Created `.kiro` workspace config with agent configuration for Next.js project
2. Regenerated all favicon and manifest icons from `public/images/logo.png`
3. Fixed Composer dependencies by deleting `composer.lock` and reinstalling with PHP 8.1
4. Installed `php81rc-pgsql` extension for PostgreSQL database connectivity
5. Fixed file ownership permissions on `~/webapps/kompaskasih-backend` (was owned by different user)
6. Created branch `fix/update-consent-and-header-copy` with UI copy updates
7. Pushed changes and created MR !3 to staging

## TECHNICAL CONTEXT
- Server: Ubuntu with RunCloud (nginx reverse proxy → Apache → PHP-FPM)
- PHP-FPM socket: `/var/run/kompaskasih-backend.sock`
- PHP 8.2 is active for kompaskasih-backend
- Laravel 10.50.2 application
- PostgreSQL database connection required
- Frontend: Next.js 14 with Tailwind CSS, Malay (Bahasa Malaysia) UI

## TOOLS EXECUTED
- `sips` for image resizing (favicon generation)
- `magick` for .ico generation
- `brew install imagemagick glab php81rc-pgsql`
- `git` for branch creation and MR
- `curl` for HTTP testing
- `ssh albert` for remote server access

## NEXT STEPS
1. Verify backend is responding correctly after permission fix
2. Merge MR !3 to staging once backend is confirmed working
3. Commit and push any additional fixes if needed

## TODO LIST
- [ ] Verify kompaskasih-backend HTTP 200 response
- [ ] Merge MR !3 to staging

## FACTUAL RECORD

### Files Modified and Read
_(Most frequently and recently accessed first. Indented items show summaries for the last 5 modifications.)_
* .kiro/agents/kompas-kasih.json - Create workspace agent config for this Next.js project
* .kiro/settings/cli.json - Create workspace CLI settings

### Commands Executed
_(Showing 20 of 97 commands, most frequently and recently executed first)_
* ssh albert "curl -s http://127.0.0.1:81/ -H 'Host: kompaskasih.getactiv.my' -w '\nHTTP Status: %{http_code}\n' 2>&1" - Test the backend again after fixing permissions
* glab mr create --source-branch chore/init-kiro-config --target-branch staging --title "chore(config): init .kiro workspace config and fix datas format... - Create the merge request to staging
* ssh albert "cd ~/webapps/kompaskasih-backend && /RunCloud/Packages/php82rc/bin/php artisan route:list --path=api 2>&1 | head -20" - Test if Laravel routes work now
* ssh albert "sudo chown -R albert:albert ~/webapps/kompaskasih-backend && ls -la ~/webapps/kompaskasih-backend/vendor/composer/ | head -10" - Fix file ownership for kompaskasih-backend
* ssh albert "sudo tail -100 /var/log/php82rc-fpm.log 2>/dev/null | grep -i error" - Check for errors in PHP-FPM log
* ssh albert "sudo tail -50 /var/log/php82rc-fpm.log 2>/dev/null" - Check recent PHP-FPM 8.2 log entries
* ssh albert "curl -sv http://127.0.0.1:81/ -H 'Host: kompaskasih.getactiv.my' 2>&1 | head -100" - Get verbose output to see the error
* ssh albert "curl -s http://127.0.0.1:81/ -H 'Host: kompaskasih.getactiv.my' 2>&1 | head -100" - Get the full error response
* ssh albert "sudo tail -100 /var/log/php82rc-fpm.log 2>/dev/null" - Check full PHP-FPM 8.2 log
* ssh albert "sudo tail -100 /var/log/php82rc-fpm.log 2>/dev/null | grep -i 'kompaskasih\|error' | tail -50" - Check PHP-FPM 8.2 error log for kompaskasih-backend
* ssh albert "sudo tail -100 /var/log/php81rc-fpm.log 2>/dev/null | grep -i 'kompaskasih\|error' | tail -50" - Check PHP-FPM error log for kompaskasih-backend
* ssh albert "sudo tail -100 /var/log/apache2-rc/error_log 2>/dev/null" - Check Apache error log
* ssh albert "sudo ls /var/log/apache2-rc/ 2>/dev/null" - List Apache log files
* ssh albert "sudo tail -100 /var/log/apache2-rc/kompaskasih-backend_error.log 2>/dev/null" - Check kompaskasih-backend Apache error log
* ssh albert "sudo tail -100 /var/log/apache2-rc/error.log 2>/dev/null" - Check Apache error log
* ssh albert "sudo ls /var/log" - List all log files with sudo
* ssh albert "ls /var/log" - List all log files
* ssh albert "ls /var/log/ 2>/dev/null" - List all log files
* ssh albert "ls /var/log/ 2>/dev/null | head -30" - List all log files
* ssh albert "ls /var/log/apache2-rc/ 2>/dev/null; echo '---'; ls /var/log/httpd/ 2>/dev/null; echo '---'; ls /var/log/ 2>/dev/null | grep -i apache" - Check Apache log directories

content.data:
The backend is now working (HTTP 200). The issue was file permissions on the vendor directory. I fixed it with `chown -R albert:albert ~/webapps/kompaskasih-backend`.

The site is now accessible at `http://kompaskasih.getactiv.my`.
we use nginx-rc

You're right — the frontend-facing server is nginx-rc, but RunCloud uses a hybrid stack where nginx-rc proxies to Apache (httpd) on port 81, which then connects to PHP-FPM.

The fix I applied (fixing file permissions) resolved the 500 error. The backend is now working.

Is there anything else you'd like me to check or fix?
can you anylize what causing the error on the pasted image
I don't see any image attached to your message. Could you please share the image or describe the error you're seeing?
[pasted image (860×1076 345.0 KB)]

The error is:

```
TypeError: inputRef.current.showPicker is not a function.
(In 'inputRef.current.showPicker()', 'inputRef.current.showPicker' is undefined)
```

**Cause:** The code is calling `inputRef.current.showPicker()` on an HTML input element, but `.showPicker()` is not supported on the user's mobile browser. This API is relatively new and not available on older iOS Safari or some Android browsers.

This is likely happening on a date input (`<input type="date">`) where the code tries to programmatically open the native date picker.

Let me find the relevant code:

input.__tool_use_purpose: Find where showPicker is used in the codebase

content.data: Found it. The fix is to add a feature check before calling `.showPicker()`. Here's the fix:

input.__tool_use_purpose: Add feature detection before calling showPicker to prevent the error on unsupported browsers

content.data: Successfully replaced 1 occurrence(s) in /Users/tnkapdy/sites/js/nextjs/prima_kompas_kasih_client/src/components/ui/floating-label-input.tsx.

tool.tool_use_purpose: Add feature detection before calling showPicker to prevent the error on unsupported browsers

content.data:
**Summary:**

The error occurs because `.showPicker()` is called without checking if the browser supports it. This API was introduced in Chrome 99+ and Safari 16+, so older mobile browsers (the user appears to be on iOS with an older Safari) don't have it.

The fix adds `typeof inputRef.current.showPicker === "function"` checks before calling it. On unsupported browsers, the input will still work — users just tap the field directly to open the native picker instead of it being triggered programmatically.

Want me to commit this fix?

commit and push hot fix

content.data:
Done. Here's what was added:

1. **`.env.example`** — added `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_ENV=local`
2. **`verify-otp-form.tsx`** — when `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_ENV === "local"`:
   - OTP inputs are prefilled with `000000`
   - A yellow info banner shows: "⚠️ OTP telah dilangkau (bypass). Kod lalai: **000000**"

On the server, set `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_ENV=local` in the `.env` file. When SMTP is working again, change it to `production` to hide the banner and remove the prefill.

# Git Workflow

## Branch Naming
```
feature/short-description     # new features
fix/short-description         # bug fixes
chore/short-description       # maintenance, deps, config
refactor/short-description    # code improvements
```

## Commit Messages (Conventional Commits)
```
<type>(<scope>): <short description>

[optional body]
```

Types: `feat`, `fix`, `chore`, `refactor`, `test`, `docs`, `perf`

Examples:
```
feat(auth): add email verification on registration
fix(orders): prevent duplicate order on double-click
refactor(users): extract service from handler
test(api): add missing auth boundary tests
```

Rules:
- Subject line ≤ 72 characters
- Use imperative mood: "add" not "added"
- Reference issue numbers in body if applicable

## Workflow
```bash
# Start a feature
git checkout main && git pull
git checkout -b feature/my-feature

# Work in small, logical commits
git add <specific-files>
git commit -m "feat(scope): description"

# Push and open PR
git push -u origin feature/my-feature
```

## PR Rules
- Title follows commit message format
- Description includes: what changed, how to test, screenshots if UI
- All tests must pass before merge
- No direct pushes to `main`
- Squash merge to keep main history clean

## What NOT to Commit
- Environment files (`.env`, `.env.local`)
- Dependency directories (`node_modules/`, `vendor/`, `venv/`, `target/`)
- IDE config (`.idea/`, `.vscode/`) — use `.gitignore`
- Build output (`dist/`, `build/`, `out/`, `public/build/`)
- Secrets or credentials of any kind
- OS files (`.DS_Store`, `Thumbs.db`)

input.__tool_use_purpose: Create production Dockerfile
input.content:
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /app

COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci

COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM node:20-alpine AS runner

WORKDIR /app

ENV NODE_ENV=production

COPY --from=builder /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder /app/.next/static ./.next/static
COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public

EXPOSE 3000

CMD ["node", "server.js"]

content.data:
Build passes. Here's the summary of changes:

**Root cause:** The server was running `next dev` (development mode) via Docker, which serves CSS with hot-reload timestamps causing the preload warnings.

**Changes made (local only):**
- `next.config.mjs` — added `output: "standalone"` for optimized production Docker builds
- `docker/production/Dockerfile` — multi-stage production build (build → standalone runner)
- `docker/production/docker-compose.yml` — production compose config
- `Makefile` — added `prod-up`, `prod-down`, `prod-build` commands

On the server, use `sudo make prod-build && sudo make prod-down && sudo make prod-up` to run the production build. This eliminates the CSS preload warnings and serves optimized assets.

```bash
cd ~/webapps/kompaskasih-frontend
git pull origin staging
sudo make prod-build
sudo make down        # stop the dev container
sudo make prod-up     # start production container
```
